name: Bank Of Stronghurst
description_short: Community bank chartered in Illinois in 1946, offering FDIC-insured deposit accounts, online/mobile banking, and agricultural lending across three locations.
description_long:
Bank of Stronghurst is an independently-owned community bank that was organized and chartered by the State of Illinois on April 24, 1946, opening for business four days later with 130 depositors and $192,000 in deposits. Over 78 years, the institution has grown from a single Main Street location to operate three banking centers across Illinois: the original Stronghurst location (expanded in 1976 and 1992), a Lomax Banking Center (opened 1996), and a Dallas Banking Center (opened 2004). The bank serves Henderson and Hancock County residents and maintains FDIC insurance coverage backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government.

Bank of Stronghurst offers a full suite of community banking services including deposit accounts (checking, savings, Christmas Club), online and mobile banking platforms, lending services for personal purchases, and specialized agricultural financing. Their agricultural lending division serves farm borrowers for land purchases, livestock, farm inputs, and equipment financing, with dedicated loan officers experienced in agricultural lending. The bank also provides financial planning resources including calculators to help customers reach their financial goals and offers account transfer assistance through a Switch Kit for customers moving from other institutions.

The bank distinguishes itself through deep agricultural expertise and 78 years of continuous local operation. Management emphasizes their stability with "steady growth, solid earnings, and exceptional capital levels," and highlights their locally-owned structure where stockholders are community members. Their specialized agricultural lending team has served farm customers for 70 years and positions itself as understanding agricultural financing needs with customized loan portfolios, competitive rates, and flexible terms. The bank's three-location footprint and personal relationship-based approach differentiates it from larger regional and national competitors.

Bank of Stronghurst is a legitimate, FDIC-insured institution with verifiable history and transparent operations. However, as a small community bank, they likely have more limited product offerings, higher minimum balances, and less competitive rates than larger banks. Their geographic service area is restricted to Illinois counties, making them inaccessible to out-of-state customers. Digital banking capabilities appear functional but are not marketed as cutting-edge, suggesting they may lag larger institutions in fintech innovation. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Agricultural/farm lending (land, livestock, equipment, farm inputs)
Telephone banking at three branch locations
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
78 years of continuous operation with documented stable growth and exceptional capital levels
Specialized agricultural lending team with 70 years of farm financing expertise and customized portfolios
Locally-owned and operated independent bank with stockholders from the community
Three physical banking locations across Henderson and Hancock County with extended hours
Mobile and online banking platforms for remote account management
Account transfer assistance through Switch Kit program for customers changing banks
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to Illinois; cannot serve out-of-state customers
No information provided on interest rates, fees, minimum balances, or product-specific terms
Small institution likely has fewer digital products and less fintech innovation than larger banks
Limited transparency on website regarding specific deposit rates, lending rates, or loan approval criteria
Only three physical locations may be inconvenient for customers outside Henderson and Hancock counties
best_for: Agricultural borrowers seeking farm loans for land, livestock, equipment, or farm inputs with personalized service
Illinois residents in Henderson and Hancock counties seeking relationship-based community banking
Customers prioritizing local ownership and depositor protection through FDIC insurance
Existing customers of the bank seeking to understand available services and switching options
diagnosis: Bank of Stronghurst is best for agricultural borrowers and Illinois residents in Henderson and Hancock counties seeking personalized community banking with FDIC protection and specialized farm financing expertise. The primary caveat is geographic restriction to a small Illinois service area and lack of transparent pricing information on deposits and loans, requiring customers to contact the bank directly for rates and terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
